heading: Use of public property
label: 7779 Use of public property
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)4779(a)4779(b)10:1332.10:1345., .June 23, 1879, ch. 35, § 821 Stat. 354779(c)10:1335.(2d par. under “Quartermaster Corps”), .Aug. 1, 1914, ch. 22338 Stat. 629(last proviso under “Quartermaster’s Department”), ; , .July 16, 1892, ch. 19527 Stat. 178June 28, 1950, ch. 383, § 402(c)64 Stat. 227In subsection (a), the words “United States” are substituted for the word “Government”.In subsection (b), the words “suitable space” are substituted for the words “proper and suitable room or rooms”. The words “there is a” are substituted for the words “have been established”.

children.label: a
children.text: When the economy of the Army so requires, the Secretary of the Army shall establish military headquarters in places where suitable buildings are owned by the United States.

children.label: b
children.text: No money appropriated for the support of the Army may be spent for post gardens or Army exchanges. However, this does not prevent Army exchanges from using public buildings or public transportation that, in the opinion of the office or officer designated by the Secretary, are not needed for other purposes.
